copilot.title: Copilot
copilot.emptyStateCopy: Your in-app assistant. It sees the record or list you're on — ask it to summarize, draft, or explain what you're looking at.
suggestions: Draft a follow-up for this record
What should I do next here?

brief.text: Brightline builds a real-time analytics platform for mid-market software teams. They came in through the platform-rollout deal and expanded fast after the first pilot landed.
Maya Verstraete (CTO) is the champion. The main open question is how deeply the rollout touches their existing data warehouse before the Q3 review.
Nordwind runs a 900-person retail operation across the Benelux. The store-copilot deal is early but the ops team is engaged and moving.
Jonas Peeters (Ops Director) owns the evaluation. Next step is a scoped pilot in two flagship stores before a wider rollout.

questions.label: Write the opening paragraph
questions.hint: You can flesh out the rest in the editor after.
questions.placeholder: A short summary to start from

body.text: The quarterly rollout happens in three phases, each gated on the previous one landing cleanly. The goal is a controlled ramp rather than a single big-bang release.
A small private cohort gets access first. We watch activation and time-to-value closely and fix the sharp edges before widening the door.
Once the beta stabilises, the waitlist converts in batches. Support load and infrastructure headroom decide the batch size.
Ship the ramp, not the big bang.

elements.text: one place for the whole rollout · updated weekly
elements.originalText: one place for the whole rollout · updated weekly

body.text: A new customer should reach their first real outcome inside the first session. Everything in this playbook works backward from that moment.
Import a starter dataset so the app is never empty
Land one visible win before the call ends
The empty state is the enemy. Seed it, and the product sells itself.
Pipeline held steady this week. Two deals advanced to Proposal and one closed, with the rest waiting on the Q3 review before they move.

sampleRows.rule: Never open with a rhetorical question.
Always name the customer's role before their company.
Never promise a delivery date we haven't confirmed.
